sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Lecce and Napoli on May 3, 2025, at 16:00 at the Via del Mare stadium in Lecce promises to be a fascinating encounter. Lecce, sitting at 17th in the league with 27 points, are desperate for points to climb away from the relegation zone. In stark contrast, Napoli lead the Serie A standings at 1st place with an impressive 74 points, showcasing their dominance this season. This Round 35 matchup, officiated by Davide Massa who averages 4.8 yellow cards per game, is set to be a test of resilience for the home side against the league leaders.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Napoli have clearly had the upper hand with 3 wins compared to Lecce's 0, alongside 2 draws. Their most recent encounter on October 26, 2024, saw Napoli edge out a 1-0 victory at home, with Giovanni Di Lorenzo earning the Player of the Match honors with an 8.3 Sofascore rating. Di Lorenzo has been a standout in these fixtures, boasting an average rating of 7.8 across 4 starts against Lecce, along with 2 goals scored. Napoli’s streak of no losses in the last 5 head-to-head games and no goals conceded in the last 3 against Lecce further underline their historical dominance.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Napoli enter this game in scintillating form, riding a streak of 3 consecutive wins and remaining unbeaten in their last 8 matches. They've also kept clean sheets in their last 3 games and have been the first to score in their last 5 matches. Lecce, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 11 games and without a clean sheet in their last 9 outings. Additionally, Lecce have been the first to concede in 5 of their last 6 matches, while Napoli have been first-half winners in 4 of their last 5 games, indicating a potential early lead for the visitors.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Napoli, Romelu Lukaku stands out with 12 goals (5th in the league) and a league-leading 10 assists, making him a constant threat. Scott McTominay, also in fine form with 11 goals and an average Sofascore rating of 8.12 in the last 5 matches, will be crucial in midfield. On Lecce’s side, Nikola Krstović leads the attack with 10 goals and an average rating of 7.14, while Kialonda Gaspar impresses defensively with a league-best 77.9% ground duels won. Medon Berisha, with a league-high 11.2% long balls in total passes, could play a pivotal role in transitioning play. Players like Santiago Pierotti, with 4 goals, and Antonino Gallo, with 2 assists, will also look to make an impact for Lecce.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Style
sections.text: Lecce’s season stats reveal struggles in attack and defense, scoring only 24 goals (20th in the league) and conceding 56 (18th for least goals conceded). However, they excel in overperforming expected goals with a difference of 8.1025 (1st in the league) and have a strong 75% of goals assisted (2nd in the league). Napoli, while not detailed in stats here, are likely far superior given their league position, and their goal distribution shows late-game strength with 28% of goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Lecce score most between the 31st and 45th minutes (28.57% of goals), which could be a key period to exploit. Lecce also average 4.32 corners per game (13th) and have hit the woodwork 10 times (12th for unluckiest), indicating some missed opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Lecce will miss defender Gaby Jean, though his impact statistically is minimal with no goals or assists. Napoli, however, face more significant absences with key midfielder Frank Anguissa (6 goals, 4 assists) sidelined, along with forward David Neres and defenders Alessandro Buongiorno and Juan Jesus. These absences could slightly dent Napoli’s usual fluidity, but their squad depth, bolstered by players like Lukaku and McTominay, should help them cope.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Lecce’s strength lies in defensive metrics like tackles per game (17.47, 3rd in the league) and clearances per game (24.76, 2nd), suggesting a strategy of sitting deep and countering. Their 51.13% ground duels won (5th) and fast breaks per game (1.18, 3rd) indicate potential for quick transitions. Napoli, with their superior possession and attacking output, are likely to dominate the ball and press high, as evidenced by their first-to-score streak in recent games. Lecce’s vulnerability in the first half could be exploited by Napoli’s early aggression.
